global.title: Collaboration management lives in a new Collaborate tab inside the existing chat panel
global.content:
## UX: Collaborate tab inside the Chat panel

### Why the chat panel?

The chat panel already owns everything collab needs:

- Peer address resolution.
- Public profile lookup (Bee pubkey for ACT).
- Multi-user picker patterns (from the existing document-share picker).
- Notification plumbing.

Adding Collaborate next to the existing conversation list keeps everything multi-peer in one place rather than scattering peer-oriented UI across the app.

### Layout

```
Chat Panel (left pane)
  ├── [Conversations] tab    ← existing
  └── [Collaborate]    tab   ← NEW
        ├── "+ New collaboration" button
        ├── Pending invitations  (I received)
        └── Active collaborations (I'm part of)
```

### Key flows

- **Create:** pick target (Drive or Document), pick participants (multi-select from known chat peers or paste a Swarm ID), optional caption, click Invite.
- **Accept:** recipient sees a `CollabInviteCard` in chat — mirrors `DocumentShareCard` but with `[Join]` instead of `[Import]`.
- **Active row:** drive/doc name, participant avatars (initials — allowed as a list, not a per-editor overlay), last activity timestamp. Click opens the drive/doc in Connect.
- **Leave:** local-only stop (feed writes/pulls stop); initiator can later remove from manifest.
global.description: The Collaborate tab is added to the existing chat panel — reuses peer resolution, public-profile lookup, multi-user picker, and notification plumbing already built for chat.
